text.content:
In agriculture, your farm equipment’s performance is the key to your operations. For agriculture tires, features like traction and flotation optimize your machines to farm to the fullest. Optimizing both traction and flotation on your tractors also reduce soil compaction, protecting crops and the land that helps them grow.

Below, we’ll jump into the best ways to optimize traction and flotation. In this helpful guide, you’ll unlock great tips to ensure your operations go on without a hitch.

rich_text.plain_text:
In agriculture, your farm equipment’s performance is the key to your operations. For agriculture tires, features like traction and flotation optimize your machines to farm to the fullest. Optimizing both traction and flotation on your tractors also reduce soil compaction, protecting crops and the land that helps them grow.

Below, we’ll jump into the best ways to optimize traction and flotation. In this helpful guide, you’ll unlock great tips to ensure your operations go on without a hitch.

text.content: Flotation vs. Traction: What's the Difference?

rich_text.plain_text: Flotation vs. Traction: What's the Difference?

text.content: Traction

rich_text.plain_text: Traction

text.content: An Ag tires capability to deliver the torque or engine power relies on traction. Traction overcomes the friction between the surface of the tractor tire and the ground. When tractors move, the engine generates torque that pushes the vehicle’s wheels or tires forward and overcomes the friction between the tire surface area and the road surface area. In summary, traction is the friction generated between the tire and the road or field surface.

rich_text.plain_text: An Ag tires capability to deliver the torque or engine power relies on traction. Traction overcomes the friction between the surface of the tractor tire and the ground. When tractors move, the engine generates torque that pushes the vehicle’s wheels or tires forward and overcomes the friction between the tire surface area and the road surface area. In summary, traction is the friction generated between the tire and the road or field surface.

text.content: Flotation

rich_text.plain_text: Flotation

text.content: Flotation is the ability of a tire to stay on the surface of soft ground, soil, or snow without digging into the surface. In agriculture, tires are designed to deliver the optimal flotation based on their size, load capability, and compounding.

rich_text.plain_text: Flotation is the ability of a tire to stay on the surface of soft ground, soil, or snow without digging into the surface. In agriculture, tires are designed to deliver the optimal flotation based on their size, load capability, and compounding.

text.content: Optimizing Traction and Flotation

rich_text.plain_text: Optimizing Traction and Flotation

text.content: In all cases, the optimum air pressure adjusted for the weight or load, and field condition (roading vs fieldwork) will ensure that the tire delivers the best possible traction and flotation.

rich_text.plain_text: In all cases, the optimum air pressure adjusted for the weight or load, and field condition (roading vs fieldwork) will ensure that the tire delivers the best possible traction and flotation.

text.content: Gross Flat Plate

rich_text.plain_text: Gross Flat Plate

text.content: Gross Flat Plate (GFP) is the surface area of a tire in contact with the ground. MAXAM provides a GFP in our catalogs based on our tires operating at 5 mph with 15 PSI or 1 Bar of Air Pressure as the initial tire footprint, assisting growers in determining their ground contact patch or pressure.

rich_text.plain_text: Gross Flat Plate (GFP) is the surface area of a tire in contact with the ground. MAXAM provides a GFP in our catalogs based on our tires operating at 5 mph with 15 PSI or 1 Bar of Air Pressure as the initial tire footprint, assisting growers in determining their ground contact patch or pressure.

text.content: MAXAM App with Ag Calculator

rich_text.plain_text: MAXAM App with Ag Calculator

text.content: The purpose of the Gross Flat Plate is to determine the ground contact patch or measured area in square inches or centimeters that at the chosen tire tread face or surface area will provide for a given load. As you increase or decrease tire air pressure the Gross Flat Plate will increase or decrease.

rich_text.plain_text: The purpose of the Gross Flat Plate is to determine the ground contact patch or measured area in square inches or centimeters that at the chosen tire tread face or surface area will provide for a given load. As you increase or decrease tire air pressure the Gross Flat Plate will increase or decrease.

text.content: Recap: Optimizing Traction and Flotation for Your Tractor

rich_text.plain_text: Recap: Optimizing Traction and Flotation for Your Tractor

text.content: To get the most out of your tractor, it’s essential to optimize both traction and flotation. Proper traction ensures efficient power transfer and movement, while good flotation reduces soil compaction, preserving your land and crop health.

rich_text.plain_text: To get the most out of your tractor, it’s essential to optimize both traction and flotation. Proper traction ensures efficient power transfer and movement, while good flotation reduces soil compaction, preserving your land and crop health.

text.content: Key Optimization Tips:

rich_text.plain_text: Key Optimization Tips:

text.content: Adjust Tire Pressure

rich_text.plain_text: Adjust Tire Pressure

text.content: : The right air pressure based on load and field conditions is critical. Lower pressure increases flotation (larger footprint), while higher pressure may improve road performance but reduce field efficiency.

rich_text.plain_text: : The right air pressure based on load and field conditions is critical. Lower pressure increases flotation (larger footprint), while higher pressure may improve road performance but reduce field efficiency.

text.content: Monitor Tire Slip

rich_text.plain_text: Monitor Tire Slip

text.content: : Use slip meters to find the ideal pressure. Too much slip? Lower the pressure or add ballast. Too little? Raise pressure or remove weights.

rich_text.plain_text: : Use slip meters to find the ideal pressure. Too much slip? Lower the pressure or add ballast. Too little? Raise pressure or remove weights.

text.content: Balance Load Distribution

rich_text.plain_text: Balance Load Distribution

text.content: : Proper front-to-rear weight balance impacts both traction and flotation.

rich_text.plain_text: : Proper front-to-rear weight balance impacts both traction and flotation.

text.content: Gross Flat Plate (GFP)

rich_text.plain_text: Gross Flat Plate (GFP)

text.content: : GFP measures tire-ground contact area. Adjusting air pressure changes GFP, which helps manage soil pressure and load distribution.

rich_text.plain_text: : GFP measures tire-ground contact area. Adjusting air pressure changes GFP, which helps manage soil pressure and load distribution.

text.content: In short, matching tire pressure, load, and application conditions will boost your tractor’s efficiency, protect your soil, and save fuel.
